terms.definition: The principle that the individual owns their body, labor, mind, time, and conscience.
The distribution of control away from single choke points and concentrated authorities.
A strategy focused on voluntary exchange, counter-economics, and building outside coercive systems.
An ethos using cryptography and code to defend privacy, speech, and individual autonomy.
Currency whose value is enforced by government decree and institutional acceptance rather than commodity backing.
The shaping of public agreement through media framing, institutional pressure, and selective narrative repetition.
